title: Hash-ID it
description:
Amaka pins four hash samples from the incident bucket onto the whiteboard and waves you over. Before anyone cracks anything, she wants to know you can read a hash on sight — length, charset, and prefix are usually enough.

  #1  5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592
  #2  aaf4c61ddcc5e8a2dabede0f3b482cd9ea86f1a1
  #3  $2b$12$R9h/cIPz0gi.URNNX3kh2OPST9/PgBkqquzi.Ss7KIUgO2t0jWMUW
  #4  2c26b46b68ffc68ff99b453c1d30413413422d706483bfa0f98a5e886266e7ae

Amaka taps the board next to sample #3: "Which format is hash #3?" Pick the algorithm from the options below.
